--- /dev/null
+++ b/scrimage-filters/src/main/java/com/sksamuel/scrimage/filter/instagram/ValenciaFilter.java
@@ -0,0 +1,15 @@
+package com.sksamuel.scrimage.filter.instagram;
+
+import java.util.Arrays;
+
+import static com.sksamuel.scrimage.filter.instagram.CssFilters.*;
+
+/**
+ * Clean-room implementation of the Instagram "valencia" filter:
+ * sepia(.25) contrast(1.1) brightness(1.1) + rgba(230,193,61,.1) lighten.
+ */
+public class ValenciaFilter extends InstagramFilter {
+   public ValenciaFilter() {
+      super(Arrays.asList(sepia(0.25f), contrast(1.1f), brightness(1.1f)), Overlay.solid(230, 193, 61, 0.1f, BlendMode.LIGHTEN));
+   }
+}
diff --git a/scrimage-filters/src/test/kotlin/com/sksamuel/scrimage/filter/instagram/ValenciaFilterTest.kt b/scrimage-filters/src/test/kotlin/com/sksamuel/scrimage/filter/instagram/ValenciaFilterTest.kt
new file mode 100644
index 00000000..98cdc2ea
--- /dev/null
+++ b/scrimage-filters/src/test/kotlin/com/sksamuel/scrimage/filter/instagram/ValenciaFilterTest.kt
@@ -0,0 +1,16 @@
+package com.sksamuel.scrimage.filter.instagram
+
+import com.sksamuel.scrimage.ImmutableImage
+import io.kotest.core.spec.style.FunSpec
+import io.kotest.matchers.shouldBe
+import io.kotest.matchers.shouldNotBe
+
+class ValenciaFilterTest : FunSpec({
+   val image = ImmutableImage.fromResource("/bird.jpg").scaleToWidth(120)
+   test("valencia preserves the image dimensions and alters the image") {
+      val out = image.filter(ValenciaFilter())
+      out.width shouldBe image.width
+      out.height shouldBe image.height
+      out shouldNotBe image
+   }
+})

author: Sam
message: Scale the watermark_stamp example with the image (#578)
patch:
commit eef34e77b15a95b0cb465237b1b4b62589a00d28
Author: Sam <sam@sksamuel.com>
Date:   Fri May 29 01:10:29 2026 -0500

    Scale the watermark_stamp example with the image (#578)
    
    WatermarkStampFilter draws the text at the literal Font size and does not
    scale it to the image, so the fixed 96px stamp font was tiny on the large
    sample image. This is not a filter bug. Size the stamp font from the
    source width (width / 10) in the example so the stamp is prominent at any
    resolution, and drop the now-unused stampFont constant.
    
    Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Sam
message: Fix gain_bias example showing no effect (#574)
patch:
commit 5805788bdd69bd9f347bb1d075be83541a7bd11a
Author: Sam <sam@sksamuel.com>
Date:   Fri May 29 00:56:29 2026 -0500

    Fix gain_bias example showing no effect (#574)
    
    The example used GainBiasFilter(0.5, 0.5), which are the filter's neutral
    values: ImageMath.bias(a, 0.5) and gain(a, 0.5) are both the identity
    transform, so the output was identical to the original. The filter itself
    is correct.
    
    Use gain 0.7 / bias 0.6 so the example shows a visible contrast/brightness
    adjustment.
    
    Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Sam
message: Fix alpha_mask example showing no effect (#570)
patch:
commit 6451ca45476db6bc70d1ca4dddbf579ccedcbd9a
Author: Sam <sam@sksamuel.com>
Date:   Fri May 29 00:51:50 2026 -0500

    Fix alpha_mask example showing no effect (#570)
    
    The alpha_mask example used AlphaMaskFilter with the default channel (0 =
    alpha), sampling the mask's alpha channel. The mask is an opaque JPEG, so
    every pixel resolved to full opacity and the result was identical to the
    original. The filter itself is correct.
    
    Drive the alpha from the mask's red channel instead (so it actually
    varies) and composite the masked image over a checkerboard so the
    resulting transparency is visible, matching the invert_alpha example.
    
    Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
---
 examples/filters/bird_alpha_mask_large.jpeg        | Bin 403671 -> 731778 bytes
 examples/filters/bird_alpha_mask_small.png         | Bin 125066 -> 92263 bytes
 .../com/sksamuel/scrimage/ExampleGenerator.scala   |   7 +++++++
 3 files changed, 7 insertions(+)

diff --git a/examples/filters/bird_alpha_mask_large.jpeg b/examples/filters/bird_alpha_mask_large.jpeg
index 280f237b..e823c0f2 100644
Binary files a/examples/filters/bird_alpha_mask_large.jpeg and b/examples/filters/bird_alpha_mask_large.jpeg differ
diff --git a/examples/filters/bird_alpha_mask_small.png b/examples/filters/bird_alpha_mask_small.png
index 68485385..1c240218 100644
Binary files a/examples/filters/bird_alpha_mask_small.png and b/examples/filters/bird_alpha_mask_small.png differ
diff --git a/scrimage-filters/src/test/scala/com/sksamuel/scrimage/ExampleGenerator.scala b/scrimage-filters/src/test/scala/com/sksamuel/scrimage/ExampleGenerator.scala
index 96c00f27..cd8e7652 100644
--- a/scrimage-filters/src/test/scala/com/sksamuel/scrimage/ExampleGenerator.scala
+++ b/scrimage-filters/src/test/scala/com/sksamuel/scrimage/ExampleGenerator.scala
@@ -169,6 +169,13 @@ object ExampleGenerator extends App {
         val w = source.width
         val faded = source.map(p => new java.awt.Color(p.red, p.green, p.blue, if (w > 1) (255 * p.x) / (w - 1) else 255))
         checkerboard(source.width, source.height).overlay(faded.filter(new InvertAlphaFilter()))
+      } else if (filterName == "alpha_mask") {
+        // AlphaMaskFilter drives the source's alpha from a channel of the mask
+        // image. Masking the (opaque) alpha channel of a JPEG mask is a no-op,
+        // so sample the mask's red channel (channel 1) for a varying alpha and
+        // composite over a checkerboard so the transparency is visible.
+        val masked = source.filter(new AlphaMaskFilter(differentFrom(imgName, source), 1))
+        checkerboard(source.width, source.height).overlay(masked)
       } else if (filterName == "noise_reduction")
         source.filter(new NoiseReductionFilter(), new NoiseReductionFilter(), new NoiseReductionFilter(), new NoiseReductionFilter(), new NoiseReductionFilter())
       else if (filterName == "blur")

Author: Sam <sam@sksamuel.com>

    Make the blur example stronger (#565)
    
    BlurFilter is a fixed 3x3 box blur with no strength parameter, so the

     println("Generating example " + imgName + " " + filterName)
     // invert_alpha only flips the alpha channel, which is invisible on an opaque
     // photo saved as JPEG: fade the source's alpha, invert, and composite over a

       if (filterName == "invert_alpha") {
         val w = source.width
@@ -171,6 +171,8 @@ object ExampleGenerator extends App {

author: Sam
message: Make the noise_reduction example more extreme (#561)
patch:
commit 51ddbf2addc36489cc6b693cc9df269151f639c6
Author: Sam <sam@sksamuel.com>
Date:   Fri May 29 00:25:18 2026 -0500

    Make the noise_reduction example more extreme (#561)
    
    NoiseReduction has no strength parameter (a fixed 20 iterations), so a
    single pass is subtle. The example now applies NoiseReductionFilter five
    times so the smoothing is clearly visible, and regenerates the example
    image.
    
    Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
---
 .../filters/colosseum_noise_reduction_large.jpeg   | Bin 239758 -> 190177 bytes
 .../filters/colosseum_noise_reduction_small.png    | Bin 93788 -> 93658 bytes
 .../com/sksamuel/scrimage/ExampleGenerator.scala   |   9 ++++++---
 3 files changed, 6 insertions(+), 3 deletions(-)

diff --git a/examples/filters/colosseum_noise_reduction_large.jpeg b/examples/filters/colosseum_noise_reduction_large.jpeg
index 4c40d467..259763fa 100644
Binary files a/examples/filters/colosseum_noise_reduction_large.jpeg and b/examples/filters/colosseum_noise_reduction_large.jpeg differ

diff --git a/scrimage-filters/src/test/scala/com/sksamuel/scrimage/ExampleGenerator.scala b/scrimage-filters/src/test/scala/com/sksamuel/scrimage/ExampleGenerator.scala
index f9adcd69..f49ebe1f 100644
--- a/scrimage-filters/src/test/scala/com/sksamuel/scrimage/ExampleGenerator.scala
+++ b/scrimage-filters/src/test/scala/com/sksamuel/scrimage/ExampleGenerator.scala
@@ -161,14 +161,17 @@ object ExampleGenerator extends App {
     val source = img.copy(java.awt.image.BufferedImage.TYPE_INT_ARGB)
     println("Generating example " + imgName + " " + filterName)
     // invert_alpha only flips the alpha channel, which is invisible on an opaque
-    // photo saved as JPEG. Demonstrate it by fading the source's alpha left-to-right,
-    // inverting, and compositing over a checkerboard so the transparency shows.
+    // photo saved as JPEG: fade the source's alpha, invert, and composite over a
+    // checkerboard so it shows. NoiseReduction has no strength parameter (20 fixed
+    // iterations), so apply it several times for a clearly visible smoothing.
     val result =
       if (filterName == "invert_alpha") {
         val w = source.width
         val faded = source.map(p => new java.awt.Color(p.red, p.green, p.blue, if (w > 1) (255 * p.x) / (w - 1) else 255))
         checkerboard(source.width, source.height).overlay(faded.filter(new InvertAlphaFilter()))
-      } else source.filter(factory(imgName, source))
+      } else if (filterName == "noise_reduction")
+        source.filter(new NoiseReductionFilter(), new NoiseReductionFilter(), new NoiseReductionFilter(), new NoiseReductionFilter(), new NoiseReductionFilter())
+      else source.filter(factory(imgName, source))
     result.output(new File("examples/filters/" + imgName + "_" + filterName + "_large.jpeg"))(JpegWriter.compression(95))
     result.scaleToWidth(thumbWidth).forWriter(PngWriter.MaxCompression)
       .write(new File("examples/filters/" + imgName + "_" + filterName + "_small.png"))

author: Sam
message: Make the invert_alpha docs example show the effect (#560)
patch: commit 3bdf8fdcc65e25431347abdb4d656c722a0c7d17 Author: Sam Date: Fri May 29 00:20:43 2026 -0500 Make the invert_alpha docs example show the effect (#560) InvertAlphaFilter only flips the alpha channel, so on an opaque photo saved as JPEG the example looked identical to the original. The generator now builds the invert_alpha example by fading the source's alpha left-to-right, inverting it, and compositing the result over a checkerboard, so the transparency change is visible. Regenerated the invert_alpha example image. Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) --- examples/filters/lanzarote_invert_alpha_large.jpeg | Bin 406467 -> 282583 bytes examples/filters/lanzarote_invert_alpha_small.png | Bin 89734 -> 104534 bytes .../com/sksamuel/scrimage/ExampleGenerator.scala | 32 +++++++++++++++++++-- 3 files changed, 30 insertions(+), 2 deletions(-) diff --git a/examples/filters/lanzarote_invert_alpha_large.jpeg b/examples/filters/lanzarote_invert_alpha_large.jpeg index 9244deb8..8453c689 100644 Binary files a/examples/filters/lanzarote_invert_alpha_large.jpeg and b/examples/filters/lanzarote_invert_alpha_large.jpeg differ diff --git a/examples/filters/lanzarote_invert_alpha_small.png b/examples/filters/lanzarote_invert_alpha_small.png index df732403..0dee2e08 100644 Binary files a/examples/filters/lanzarote_invert_alpha_small.png and b/examples/filters/lanzarote_invert_alpha_small.png differ diff --git a/scrimage-filters/src/test/scala/com/sksamuel/scrimage/ExampleGenerator.scala b/scrimage-filters/src/test/scala/com/sksamuel/scrimage/ExampleGenerator.scala index b1930782..d6d9924c 100644 --- a/scrimage-filters/src/test/scala/com/sksamuel/scrimage/ExampleGenerator.scala +++ b/scrimage-filters/src/test/scala/com/sksamuel/scrimage/ExampleGenerator.scala @@ -129,6 +129,25 @@ object ExampleGenerator extends App { def cell(imgName: String, name: String): String = s"" + // A grey/white checkerboard, used as a backdrop so transparency is visible. + def checkerboard(w: Int, h: Int): ImmutableImage = { + val img = ImmutableImage.create(w, h) + val g = img.awt().getGraphics.asInstanceOf[java.awt.Graphics2D] + val sq = Math.max(8, w / 24) + var y = 0 + while (y < h) { + var x = 0 + while (x < w) { + g.setColor(if (((x / sq) + (y / sq)) % 2 == 0) new java.awt.Color(0xCCCCCC) else java.awt.Color.WHITE) + g.fillRect(x, y, sq, sq) + x += sq + } + y += sq + } + g.dispose() + img + } + val sb = new StringBuffer() sb.append("| Filter | Original | Filter |\n") sb.append("| ------ | -------- | ------ |\n") @@ -139,8 +158,17 @@ object ExampleGenerator extends App { // original dimensions and only the inline thumbnail is downscaled. val source = img.copy(java.awt.image.BufferedImage.TYPE_INT_ARGB) println("Generating example " + imgName + " " + filterName) - source.filter(factory(imgName, source)).output(new File("examples/filters/" + imgName + "_" + filterName + "_large.jpeg"))(JpegWriter.compression(95)) - source.filter(factory(imgName, source)).scaleToWidth(thumbWidth).forWriter(PngWriter.MaxCompression) + // invert_alpha only flips the alpha channel, which is invisible on an opaque + // photo saved as JPEG. Demonstrate it by fading the source's alpha left-to-right, + // inverting, and compositing over a checkerboard so the transparency shows. + val result = + if (filterName == "invert_alpha") { + val w = source.width + val faded = source.map(p => new java.awt.Color(p.red, p.green, p.blue, if (w > 1) (255 * p.x) / (w - 1) else 255)) + checkerboard(source.width, source.height).overlay(faded.filter(new InvertAlphaFilter())) + } else source.filter(factory(imgName, source)) + result.output(new File("examples/filters/" + imgName + "_" + filterName + "_large.jpeg"))(JpegWriter.compression(95)) + result.scaleToWidth(thumbWidth).forWriter(PngWriter.MaxCompression) .write(new File("examples/filters/" + imgName + "_" + filterName + "_small.png")) sb.append(s"| $filterName | ${cell(imgName, "original")} | ${cell(imgName, filterName)} |\n") }

author: Sam
message: Generate filter examples at the original image size (#559)
patch:
commit 95edfd5f97f264bb39cbc128e2e1d3ed809405eb
Author: Sam <sam@sksamuel.com>
Date:   Thu May 28 23:28:42 2026 -0500

    Generate filter examples at the original image size (#559)
    
    The example generator previously scaled every source down to 1200px wide
    before filtering. Use the full-size original instead, so the large
    click-through example images retain the source's original dimensions.
    The inline table thumbnails are still downscaled (to thumbWidth) for
    display.
    
    Note: the committed example images will be regenerated at full size the
    next time the generator is run.
    
    Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
---
 .../test/scala/com/sksamuel/scrimage/ExampleGenerator.scala  | 12 +++++++-----
 1 file changed, 7 insertions(+), 5 deletions(-)

diff --git a/scrimage-filters/src/test/scala/com/sksamuel/scrimage/ExampleGenerator.scala b/scrimage-filters/src/test/scala/com/sksamuel/scrimage/ExampleGenerator.scala
index b4658c6b..1dce0e4d 100644
--- a/scrimage-filters/src/test/scala/com/sksamuel/scrimage/ExampleGenerator.scala
+++ b/scrimage-filters/src/test/scala/com/sksamuel/scrimage/ExampleGenerator.scala
@@ -114,11 +114,11 @@ object ExampleGenerator extends App {
     ("watermark_stamp", (_, _) => new WatermarkStampFilter("watermark", font, true, 0.2, Color.White.toAWT))
   ).sortBy(_._1)
 
-  // One unfiltered "original" thumbnail per sample image.
+  // One unfiltered "original" thumbnail per sample image. The large click-through
+  // image is the full-size original; only the inline thumbnail is downscaled.
   for ((name, img) <- images) {
-    val r = img.scaleToWidth(1200)
-    r.output(new File("examples/filters/" + name + "_original_large.jpeg"))(JpegWriter.compression(95))
-    r.scaleToWidth(thumbWidth).forWriter(PngWriter.MaxCompression)
+    img.output(new File("examples/filters/" + name + "_original_large.jpeg"))(JpegWriter.compression(95))
+    img.scaleToWidth(thumbWidth).forWriter(PngWriter.MaxCompression)
       .write(new File("examples/filters/" + name + "_original_small.png"))
   }
 
@@ -133,7 +133,9 @@ object ExampleGenerator extends App {
 
   filters.zipWithIndex.foreach { case ((filterName, factory), i) =>
     val (imgName, img) = images(i % images.size)
-    val source = img.scaleToWidth(1200).copy(java.awt.image.BufferedImage.TYPE_INT_ARGB)
+    // Filter the full-size original; the large click-through image keeps the
+    // original dimensions and only the inline thumbnail is downscaled.
+    val source = img.copy(java.awt.image.BufferedImage.TYPE_INT_ARGB)
     println("Generating example " + imgName + " " + filterName)
     source.filter(factory(imgName, source)).output(new File("examples/filters/" + imgName + "_" + filterName + "_large.jpeg"))(JpegWriter.compression(95))
     source.filter(factory(imgName, source)).scaleToWidth(thumbWidth).forWriter(PngWriter.MaxCompression)

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) --- docs/images/{repeater_input1.jpeg => tile_input1.jpeg} | Bin docs/images/{repeater_output1.png => tile_output1.png} | Bin docs/transforms.md | 12 ++++++------ .../filter/{RepeaterFilter.java => TileFilter.java} | 6 +++--- .../filter/{RepeaterFilterTest.kt => TileFilterTest.kt} | 14 +++++++------- 5 files changed, 16 insertions(+), 16 deletions(-) diff --git a/docs/images/repeater_input1.jpeg b/docs/images/tile_input1.jpeg similarity index 100% rename from docs/images/repeater_input1.jpeg rename to docs/images/tile_input1.jpeg diff --git a/docs/images/repeater_output1.png b/docs/images/tile_output1.png similarity index 100% rename from docs/images/repeater_output1.png rename to docs/images/tile_output1.png diff --git a/docs/transforms.md b/docs/transforms.md index b5d47032..47add56f 100644 --- a/docs/transforms.md +++ b/docs/transforms.md @@ -60,28 +60,28 @@ Produces a linear gradient copy of the image for the two most dominent colours o | | | -### Repeater +### Tile -Tiles the source image into a larger image. `RepeaterFilter(columns, rows)` produces an image `columns` times the source width and `rows` times the source height, with the source drawn into every cell — so `RepeaterFilter(3, 2)` repeats the image 3 across and 2 down. +Tiles the source image into a larger image. `TileFilter(columns, rows)` produces an image `columns` times the source width and `rows` times the source height, with the source drawn into every cell — so `TileFilter(3, 2)` repeats the image 3 across and 2 down. === "Java" ``` - ImmutableImage transformed = image1.transform(new RepeaterFilter(3, 2)) + ImmutableImage transformed = image1.transform(new TileFilter(3, 2)) ``` === "Kotlin" ``` - val transformed = image1.transform(RepeaterFilter(3, 2)) + val transformed = image1.transform(TileFilter(3, 2)) ``` === "Scala" ``` - val transformed = image1.transform(new RepeaterFilter(3, 2)) + val transformed = image1.transform(new TileFilter(3, 2)) ``` | Input | Output | | ------ | --------- | -| | | +| | | diff --git a/scrimage-filters/src/main/java/com/sksamuel/scrimage/filter/RepeaterFilter.java b/scrimage-filters/src/main/java/com/sksamuel/scrimage/filter/TileFilter.java similarity index 91% rename from scrimage-filters/src/main/java/com/sksamuel/scrimage/filter/RepeaterFilter.java rename to scrimage-filters/src/main/java/com/sksamuel/scrimage/filter/TileFilter.java index 40e06d36..84850b69 100644 --- a/scrimage-filters/src/main/java/com/sksamuel/scrimage/filter/RepeaterFilter.java +++ b/scrimage-filters/src/main/java/com/sksamuel/scrimage/filter/TileFilter.java @@ -26,19 +26,19 @@ import java.awt.Graphics2D; * the source width and {@code rows} times the source height, with the source * drawn at full size into every cell. *

- * For example {@code new RepeaterFilter(2, 3)} produces an image twice as wide + * For example {@code new TileFilter(2, 3)} produces an image twice as wide * and three times as tall as the source, tiled 2 across and 3 down. *

* Because the result changes the image dimensions it is a {@link Transform} * (applied via {@code image.transform(...)}) rather than a size-preserving * {@link Filter}. */ -public class RepeaterFilter implements Transform { +public class TileFilter implements Transform { private final int columns; private final int rows; - public RepeaterFilter(int columns, int rows) { + public TileFilter(int columns, int rows) { if (columns < 1 || rows < 1) throw new IllegalArgumentException("columns and rows must both be >= 1"); this.columns = columns; diff --git a/scrimage-filters/src/test/kotlin/com/sksamuel/scrimage/filter/RepeaterFilterTest.kt b/scrimage-filters/src/test/kotlin/com/sksamuel/scrimage/filter/TileFilterTest.kt similarity index 71% rename from scrimage-filters/src/test/kotlin/com/sksamuel/scrimage/filter/RepeaterFilterTest.kt rename to scrimage-filters/src/test/kotlin/com/sksamuel/scrimage/filter/TileFilterTest.kt index d0ee7662..7fa031c5 100644 --- a/scrimage-filters/src/test/kotlin/com/sksamuel/scrimage/filter/RepeaterFilterTest.kt +++ b/scrimage-filters/src/test/kotlin/com/sksamuel/scrimage/filter/TileFilterTest.kt @@ -6,11 +6,11 @@ import io.kotest.assertions.throwables.shouldThrow import io.kotest.core.spec.style.FunSpec import io.kotest.matchers.shouldBe -class RepeaterFilterTest : FunSpec({ +class TileFilterTest : FunSpec({ - test("RepeaterFilter enlarges the image to columns x rows of the source") { + test("TileFilter enlarges the image to columns x rows of the source") { val image = ImmutableImage.create(40, 30) - val out = image.transform(RepeaterFilter(2, 3)) + val out = image.transform(TileFilter(2, 3)) out.width shouldBe 80 out.height shouldBe 90 } @@ -24,7 +24,7 @@ class RepeaterFilterTest : FunSpec({ Pixel(1, 1, 255, 255, 0, 255) ) val image = ImmutableImage.create(2, 2, pixels) - val out = image.transform(RepeaterFilter(2, 3)) + val out = image.transform(TileFilter(2, 3)) out.width shouldBe 4 out.height shouldBe 6 // top-left pixel of every tile equals the source top-left (red) @@ -36,8 +36,8 @@ class RepeaterFilterTest : FunSpec({ } } - test("RepeaterFilter rejects non-positive grid dimensions") { - shouldThrow { RepeaterFilter(0, 2) } - shouldThrow { RepeaterFilter(2, 0) } + test("TileFilter rejects non-positive grid dimensions") { + shouldThrow { TileFilter(0, 2) } + shouldThrow { TileFilter(2, 0) } } })

Every filter is listed alphabetically (including skeleton, split_channels and the previously omitted alpha_mask, background_blend, caustics, dissolve, error_spotter, laplace, noise_reduction, salt_and_pepper, watermark). Filters needing a second same-size image size against the row's image; the source is converted to TYPE_INT_ARGB so int-only filters work. Thumbnails display at width=300 (+20%). ShearFilter is omitted (it throws through the same-size filter() pipeline). author: Sam
message: Fix scaleHeightToRatio computing new height from width (#488)
patch:
commit b641b654dddf44170153de0e87327585b7ca9f2f
Author: Sam <sam@sksamuel.com>
Date:   Thu May 28 22:38:37 2026 -0500

    Fix scaleHeightToRatio computing new height from width (#488)
    
    `scaleHeightToRatio(ratio)` computed the target height as
    `(int)(width * ratio)` instead of `(int)(height * ratio)`. On a
    non-square image the result was unrelated to the input height.
    For example, on a 400×200 image, scaleHeightToRatio(0.5) returned
    an image of height 200 (= width * 0.5) — unchanged — rather than
    the expected height 100.
    
    Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
---
 .../java/com/sksamuel/scrimage/ImmutableImage.java |  2 +-
 .../scrimage/core/ops/ScaleHeightToRatioTest.kt    | 33 ++++++++++++++++++++++
 2 files changed, 34 insertions(+), 1 deletion(-)

diff --git a/scrimage-core/src/main/java/com/sksamuel/scrimage/ImmutableImage.java b/scrimage-core/src/main/java/com/sksamuel/scrimage/ImmutableImage.java
index 01f9402a..3b74bd3d 100644
--- a/scrimage-core/src/main/java/com/sksamuel/scrimage/ImmutableImage.java
+++ b/scrimage-core/src/main/java/com/sksamuel/scrimage/ImmutableImage.java
@@ -1360,7 +1360,7 @@ public class ImmutableImage extends MutableImage {
    }
 
    public ImmutableImage scaleHeightToRatio(double ratio, ScaleMethod scaleMethod) {
-      return scaleToHeight((int) (width * ratio), scaleMethod);
+      return scaleToHeight((int) (height * ratio), scaleMethod);
    }
 
    public ImmutableImage scale(double scaleFactor) {
diff --git a/scrimage-tests/src/test/kotlin/com/sksamuel/scrimage/core/ops/ScaleHeightToRatioTest.kt b/scrimage-tests/src/test/kotlin/com/sksamuel/scrimage/core/ops/ScaleHeightToRatioTest.kt
new file mode 100644
index 00000000..8aaf684b
--- /dev/null
+++ b/scrimage-tests/src/test/kotlin/com/sksamuel/scrimage/core/ops/ScaleHeightToRatioTest.kt
@@ -0,0 +1,33 @@
+package com.sksamuel.scrimage.core.ops
+
+import com.sksamuel.scrimage.ImmutableImage
+import io.kotest.core.spec.style.FunSpec
+import io.kotest.matchers.shouldBe
+
+/**
+ * Regression: scaleHeightToRatio computed the new height from
+ * `width * ratio` instead of `height * ratio`. On a non-square image
+ * the result therefore depended on the wrong dimension and produced
+ * an unrelated height. For example, on a 400 x 200 image,
+ * `scaleHeightToRatio(0.5)` returned an image of height 200 (= width
+ * * 0.5) — completely unchanged — rather than the expected height 100.
+ */
+class ScaleHeightToRatioTest : FunSpec({
+
+   test("scaleHeightToRatio(0.5) halves the height of a non-square image") {
+      val img = ImmutableImage.create(400, 200)
+      val scaled = img.scaleHeightToRatio(0.5)
+      scaled.height shouldBe 100
+   }
+
+   test("scaleHeightToRatio(2.0) doubles the height of a non-square image") {
+      val img = ImmutableImage.create(400, 200)
+      val scaled = img.scaleHeightToRatio(2.0)
+      scaled.height shouldBe 400
+   }
+
+   test("scaleHeightToRatio(1.0) preserves the height") {
+      val img = ImmutableImage.create(400, 200)
+      img.scaleHeightToRatio(1.0).height shouldBe 200
+   }
+})

author: Sam
message: Add SplitChannelsFilter (#546)
patch:
commit 4a5a5cb6181c7f24783e161cfdf20dfc2f8ee819 Author: Sam Date: Thu May 28 22:38:23 2026 -0500 Add SplitChannelsFilter (#546) SplitChannelsFilter(red, green, blue) keeps each colour channel when its flag is true and zeroes it otherwise, always preserving alpha. For example (true, false, false) isolates the red channel. Wired into ExampleGenerator and added to the filters docs table with generated example images (red-only configuration). +++++++++++++++++++++ .../scrimage/filter/SplitChannelsFilterTest.kt | 44 +++++++++++++++++ .../com/sksamuel/scrimage/ExampleGenerator.scala | 1 + 10 files changed, 101 insertions(+) diff --git a/examples/filters/bird_split_channels_small.png b/examples/filters/bird_split_channels_small.png new file mode 100644 index 00000000..b67b01ab Binary files /dev/null and b/examples/filters/bird_split_channels_small.png differ diff --git a/examples/filters/colosseum_split_channels_large.jpeg b/examples/filters/colosseum_split_channels_large.jpeg new file mode 100644 index 00000000..10ad818f Binary files /dev/null and b/examples/filters/colosseum_split_channels_large.jpeg differ diff --git a/examples/filters/colosseum_split_channels_small.png b/examples/filters/colosseum_split_channels_small.png new file mode 100644 index 00000000..33874cdf Binary files /dev/null and b/examples/filters/colosseum_split_channels_small.png differ diff --git a/examples/filters/lanzarote_split_channels_large.jpeg b/examples/filters/lanzarote_split_channels_large.jpeg new file mode 100644 index 00000000..24c61f64 Binary files /dev/null and b/examples/filters/lanzarote_split_channels_large.jpeg differ diff --git a/examples/filters/lanzarote_split_channels_small.png b/examples/filters/lanzarote_split_channels_small.png new file mode 100644 index 00000000..383546a2 Binary files /dev/null and b/examples/filters/lanzarote_split_channels_small.png differ diff --git a/scrimage-filters/src/main/java/com/sksamuel/scrimage/filter/SplitChannelsFilter.java b/scrimage-filters/src/main/java/com/sksamuel/scrimage/filter/SplitChannelsFilter.java new file mode 100644 index 00000000..8d476b47 --- /dev/null +++ b/scrimage-filters/src/main/java/com/sksamuel/scrimage/filter/SplitChannelsFilter.java @@ -0,0 +1,55 @@ +/* + Copyright 2013 Stephen K Samuel + + Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"); + you may not use this file except in compliance with the License. + You may obtain a copy of the License at + + http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0 + + Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software + distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS, + WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied. + See the License for the specific language governing permissions and + limitations under the License. + */ +package com.sksamuel.scrimage.filter; + +import com.sksamuel.scrimage.ImmutableImage; + +/** + * Keeps or removes individual colour channels. Each of the red, green and blue + * flags determines whether that channel is retained (true) or zeroed out + * (false). The alpha channel is always preserved. + *

+ * For example {@code new SplitChannelsFilter(true, false, false)} keeps only the + * red channel, producing the red component of the image. + */ +public class SplitChannelsFilter implements Filter { + + private final boolean red; + private final boolean green; + private final boolean blue; + + public SplitChannelsFilter(boolean red, boolean green, boolean blue) { + this.red = red; + this.green = green; + this.blue = blue; + } + + @Override + public void apply(ImmutableImage image) { + int w = image.width; + int h = image.height; + int[] argb = image.awt().getRGB(0, 0, w, h, null, 0, w); + // Preserve alpha; keep each colour channel only if its flag is set. + int mask = 0xFF000000 + | (red ? 0x00FF0000 : 0) + | (green ? 0x0000FF00 : 0) + | (blue ? 0x000000FF : 0); + for (int i = 0; i < argb.length; i++) { + argb[i] &= mask; + } + image.awt().setRGB(0, 0, w, h, argb, 0, w); + } +} diff --git a/scrimage-filters/src/test/kotlin/com/sksamuel/scrimage/filter/SplitChannelsFilterTest.kt b/scrimage-filters/src/test/kotlin/com/sksamuel/scrimage/filter/SplitChannelsFilterTest.kt new file mode 100644 index 00000000..22fbc96c --- /dev/null +++ b/scrimage-filters/src/test/kotlin/com/sksamuel/scrimage/filter/SplitChannelsFilterTest.kt @@ -0,0 +1,44 @@ +package com.sksamuel.scrimage.filter + +import com.sksamuel.scrimage.ImmutableImage +import com.sksamuel.scrimage.pixels.Pixel +import io.kotest.core.spec.style.FunSpec +import io.kotest.matchers.shouldBe + +class SplitChannelsFilterTest : FunSpec({ + + val pixels = arrayOf(Pixel(0, 0, 200, 150, 100, 220)) + val image = ImmutableImage.create(1, 1, pixels) + + test("keeps only the red channel when red is the only true flag") { + val p = image.filter(SplitChannelsFilter(true, false, false)).pixel(0, 0) + p.red() shouldBe 200 + p.green() shouldBe 0 + p.blue() shouldBe 0 + p.alpha() shouldBe 220 + } + + test("keeps only green and blue when red is false") { + val p = image.filter(SplitChannelsFilter(false, true, true)).pixel(0, 0) + p.red() shouldBe 0 + p.green() shouldBe 150 + p.blue() shouldBe 100 + p.alpha() shouldBe 220 + } + + test("all true leaves the colour channels unchanged") { + val p = image.filter(SplitChannelsFilter(true, true, true)).pixel(0, 0) + p.red() shouldBe 200 + p.green() shouldBe 150 + p.blue() shouldBe 100 + p.alpha() shouldBe 220 + } + + test("all false zeroes the colour channels but keeps alpha") { + val p = image.filter(SplitChannelsFilter(false, false, false)).pixel(0, 0) + p.red() shouldBe 0 + p.green() shouldBe 0 + p.blue() shouldBe 0 + p.alpha() shouldBe 220 + } +}) diff --git a/scrimage-filters/src/test/scala/com/sksamuel/scrimage/ExampleGenerator.scala b/scrimage-filters/src/test/scala/com/sksamuel/scrimage/ExampleGenerator.scala index fd50a60a..969aeedf 100644 --- a/scrimage-filters/src/test/scala/com/sksamuel/scrimage/ExampleGenerator.scala +++ b/scrimage-filters/src/test/scala/com/sksamuel/scrimage/ExampleGenerator.scala @@ -74,6 +74,7 @@ object ExampleGenerator extends App { ("sobels", new SobelsFilter), ("solarize", new SolarizeFilter), TelevisionFilter),

The image dimensions are unchanged. + */ +public class MirrorFilter implements Filter { + + @Override + public void apply(ImmutableImage image) { + int w = image.width; + int h = image.height; + int[] argb = image.awt().getRGB(0, 0, w, h, null, 0, w); + int half = w / 2; + for (int y = 0; y < h; y++) { + int row = y * w; + for (int x = 0; x < half; x++) { + int left = row + x; + int right = row + 255) } + val image = ImmutableImage.create(3, 2, pixels) + + val mirrored = image.filter(MirrorFilter()) + + mirrored.width shouldBe 3 + mirrored.height shouldBe 2 + // each row's columns are reversed: col0<->col2, col1 unchanged + for (y in 0 until 2) { + mirrored.pixel(0, y).red() shouldBe 160 + mirrored.pixel(1, y).red() shouldBe 80 + mirrored.pixel(2, y).red() shouldBe 0 + } + } + + test("MirrorFilter applied twice returns the original") { + val pixels = Array(20) { i -> Pixel(i % 5, i / 5, i * 10, i, 0, 255) } + val image = ImmutableImage.create(5, 4, pixels) + val twice = image.filter(MirrorFilter()).filter(MirrorFilter()) + twice shouldBe image + } +}) diff --git a/scrimage-filters/src/test/scala/com/sksamuel/scrimage/ExampleGenerator.scala author: Sam
message: Fix admonition syntax for the filters note callout (#544)
patch:
commit a67bc3ef7ece83d1048ca45a8359f535937a2858
Author: Sam <sam@sksamuel.com>
Date:   Thu May 28 22:09:02 2026 -0500

    Fix admonition syntax for the filters note callout (#544)
    
    The MkDocs Material `admonition` extension treats text on the same line
    as `!!! note` as the (optional) title, so
    
        !!! note These filters require the `scrimage-filters` module ...
    
    rendered as a note box titled with that sentence and an empty body.
    Move the content onto an indented line below the marker so it renders as
    the note body.
    
    Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
---
 docs/filters.md | 4 +++-
 1 file changed, 3 insertions(+), 1 deletion(-)

diff --git a/docs/filters.md b/docs/filters.md
index d5a4b66a..23bcbcd7 100644
--- a/docs/filters.md
+++ b/docs/filters.md
@@ -6,7 +6,9 @@ Scrimage comes with a wide array of filters. A filter modifies the pixels of an
 Most of these filters I have not written myself, but rather collected from other open source imaging libraries (for
 compliance with licenses and / or attribution - see file headers), and either wrapped them, fixed bugs or improved them.
 
-!!! note These filters require the `scrimage-filters` module to be added to your build.
+!!! note
+
+    These filters require the `scrimage-filters` module to be added to your build.
 
 Applying a filter returns a new image. The original image is unmodified.
 Some filters have options which can be set when creating the filters.

author: Sam
message: Respect per-pixel source alpha in BlendComposite (#523)
patch:
commit 7da37c048fb9a379d13f3d7be117d85824cdca19
Author: Sam <sam@sksamuel.com>
Date:   Thu May 28 21:56:31 2026 -0500

    Respect per-pixel source alpha in BlendComposite (#523)
    
    The blend modes derive the result purely from the colour channels and ignore
    srcPixel[3]. The final mix used only the global composite opacity, so a fully
    or partially transparent overlay pixel (alpha 0, often holding black/garbage
    RGB) still dragged the destination toward its colour — e.g. a MULTIPLY overlay
    with a transparent region darkened the base toward black there.
    
    Scale the per-pixel mix factor by the source alpha (alpha * src[3]/255). Fully
    transparent overlay pixels become no-ops; fully opaque overlays (src alpha 255)
    are identical to before.
    
    Verified: an opaque overlay produces a byte-identical result; a fully
    transparent overlay now leaves the base unchanged (it did not before).
    
    Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
---
 .../main/java/thirdparty/romainguy/BlendComposite.java | 18 +++++++++++++-----
 1 file changed, 13 insertions(+), 5 deletions(-)

diff --git a/scrimage-core/src/main/java/thirdparty/romainguy/BlendComposite.java b/scrimage-core/src/main/java/thirdparty/romainguy/BlendComposite.java
index 0f8bed40..794e17c8 100644
--- a/scrimage-core/src/main/java/thirdparty/romainguy/BlendComposite.java
+++ b/scrimage-core/src/main/java/thirdparty/romainguy/BlendComposite.java
@@ -146,11 +146,19 @@ public final class BlendComposite implements Composite {
 
                int[] result = blender.blend(srcPixel, dstPixel);
 
-               // mixes the result with the opacity
-               dstPixels[x] = ((int) (dstPixel[3] + (result[3] - dstPixel[3]) * alpha) & 0xFF) << 24 |
-                  ((int) (dstPixel[0] + (result[0] - dstPixel[0]) * alpha) & 0xFF) << 16 |
-                  ((int) (dstPixel[1] + (result[1] - dstPixel[1]) * alpha) & 0xFF) << 8 |
-                  (int) (dstPixel[2] + (result[2] - dstPixel[2]) * alpha) & 0xFF;
+               // Mix the blended result with the destination, scaling the
+               // composite opacity by the source pixel's own alpha. The blend
+               // formulas above derive the result purely from the colour
+               // channels and ignore srcPixel[3], so without this a transparent
+               // overlay pixel (alpha 0, often holding black/garbage RGB) would
+               // still drag the destination toward its colour. Scaling by
+               // src alpha makes fully transparent overlay pixels no-ops and
+               // leaves fully opaque overlays (src alpha 255) identical to before.
+               float fa = alpha * (srcPixel[3] / 255f);
+               dstPixels[x] = ((int) (dstPixel[3] + (result[3] - dstPixel[3]) * fa) & 0xFF) << 24 |
+                  ((int) (dstPixel[0] + (result[0] - dstPixel[0]) * fa) & 0xFF) << 16 |
+                  ((int) (dstPixel[1] + (result[1] - dstPixel[1]) * fa) & 0xFF) << 8 |
+                  (int) (dstPixel[2] + (result[2] - dstPixel[2]) * fa) & 0xFF;
             }
             dstOut.setDataElements(0, y, width, 1, dstPixels);
          }

author: Sam
message: Preserve image type in subimage instead of forcing ARGB (#528)
patch:
commit 1fa7673a2107940d23c5f88f8349336b9212360a
Author: Sam <sam@sksamuel.com>
Date:   Thu May 28 21:27:25 2026 -0500

    Preserve image type in subimage instead of forcing ARGB (#528)
    
    * Preserve image type in subimage instead of forcing ARGB
    
    subimage hardcoded the result type to DEFAULT_DATA_TYPE (TYPE_INT_ARGB), so
    cropping a non-ARGB image — and trim/autocrop/takeLeft/takeRight/takeTop/
    takeBottom, which all delegate here — silently promoted e.g. a TYPE_INT_RGB or
    TYPE_3BYTE_BGR image to TYPE_INT_ARGB, changing getType() downstream.
    
    Create the result with the source image's own type, falling back to the default
    ARGB type only for TYPE_CUSTOM (0), which can't be passed to the BufferedImage
    type constructor. Pixel values are unchanged (getRGB/setRGB round-trips
    losslessly for the int, 3-byte, 4-byte and gray types; verified for ARGB, RGB
    and 3BYTE_BGR sources).
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * Add tests for subimage preserving the source image type
    
    Covers the subimage type-preservation fix:
    - subimage of TYPE_INT_ARGB / TYPE_INT_RGB / TYPE_3BYTE_BGR sources keeps
      the source type and identical pixel values
    - a delegating op (takeLeft) likewise preserves the type
    - TYPE_CUSTOM sources fall back to the default ARGB type, since
      TYPE_CUSTOM can't be passed to the BufferedImage type constructor
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    ---------
    
    Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
---
 .../java/com/sksamuel/scrimage/ImmutableImage.java |  8 ++-
 .../com/sksamuel/scrimage/core/SubimageTest.kt     | 62 ++++++++++++++++++++++
 2 files changed, 69 insertions(+), 1 deletion(-)

diff --git a/scrimage-core/src/main/java/com/sksamuel/scrimage/ImmutableImage.java b/scrimage-core/src/main/java/com/sksamuel/scrimage/ImmutableImage.java
index 8dff101c..f031791e 100644
--- a/scrimage-core/src/main/java/com/sksamuel/scrimage/ImmutableImage.java
+++ b/scrimage-core/src/main/java/com/sksamuel/scrimage/ImmutableImage.java
@@ -1513,7 +1513,13 @@ public class ImmutableImage extends MutableImage {
       // to materialise a Pixel[w*h] just so wrapPixels → create(Pixel[])
       // could re-extract argb from each Pixel.
       int[] argb = awt().getRGB(x, y, w, h, null, 0, w);
-      ImmutableImage result = ImmutableImage.create(w, h, DEFAULT_DATA_TYPE);
+      // Preserve the source image's type so cropping (and trim/autocrop/takeX,
+      // which delegate here) doesn't silently promote e.g. a TYPE_INT_RGB image
+      // to TYPE_INT_ARGB. TYPE_CUSTOM (0) can't be fed to the BufferedImage type
+      // constructor, so fall back to the default ARGB type for it.
+      int type = awt().getType();
+      if (type == BufferedImage.TYPE_CUSTOM) type = DEFAULT_DATA_TYPE;
+      ImmutableImage result = ImmutableImage.create(w, h, type);
       result.awt().setRGB(0, 0, w, h, argb, 0, w);
       return result.associateMetadata(metadata);
    }
diff --git a/scrimage-tests/src/test/kotlin/com/sksamuel/scrimage/core/SubimageTest.kt b/scrimage-tests/src/test/kotlin/com/sksamuel/scrimage/core/SubimageTest.kt
index 46794939..ede21f31 100644
--- a/scrimage-tests/src/test/kotlin/com/sksamuel/scrimage/core/SubimageTest.kt
+++ b/scrimage-tests/src/test/kotlin/com/sksamuel/scrimage/core/SubimageTest.kt
@@ -5,8 +5,13 @@ import com.sksamuel.scrimage.metadata.ImageMetadata
 import com.sksamuel.scrimage.pixels.Pixel
 import io.kotest.core.spec.style.FunSpec
 import io.kotest.matchers.shouldBe
+import java.awt.Color
 import java.awt.Rectangle
+import java.awt.Transparency
+import java.awt.color.ColorSpace
 import java.awt.image.BufferedImage
+import java.awt.image.ComponentColorModel
+import java.awt.image.DataBuffer
 
 /**
  * Pin-down tests for ImmutableImage.subimage after the int[]-direct
@@ -71,4 +76,61 @@ class SubimageTest : FunSpec({
       // Empty metadata is the same singleton; just check it's not null
       sub.metadata shouldBe ImageMetadata.empty
    }
+
+   test("subimage preserves the source image type and pixel values") {
+      // The crop must come back as the same AWT type as the source rather than
+      // being silently promoted to TYPE_INT_ARGB.
+      for (type in listOf(
+         BufferedImage.TYPE_INT_ARGB,
+         BufferedImage.TYPE_INT_RGB,
+         BufferedImage.TYPE_3BYTE_BGR
+      )) {
+         val buffered = BufferedImage(4, 4, type)
+         for (y in 0 until 4)
+            for (x in 0 until 4)
+               buffered.setRGB(x, y, Color(x * 16, y * 16, 32).rgb)
+         val image = ImmutableImage.wrapAwt(buffered)
+         image.type shouldBe type
+
+         val sub = image.subimage(1, 1, 2, 2)
+         sub.type shouldBe type
+         // sub (0,0) is source (1,1); RGB values round-trip losslessly for all these types
+         sub.pixel(0, 0).red() shouldBe 16
+         sub.pixel(0, 0).green() shouldBe 16
+         sub.pixel(0, 0).blue() shouldBe 32
+         // sub (1,1) is source (2,2)
+         sub.pixel(1, 1).red() shouldBe 32
+         sub.pixel(1, 1).green() shouldBe 32
+      }
+   }
+
+   test("ops that delegate to subimage (e.g. takeLeft) also preserve the type") {
+      val buffered = BufferedImage(4, 4, BufferedImage.TYPE_INT_RGB)
+      for (y in 0 until 4)
+         for (x in 0 until 4)
+            buffered.setRGB(x, y, Color(x * 16, y * 16, 32).rgb)
+      val image = ImmutableImage.wrapAwt(buffered)
+      image.takeLeft(2).type shouldBe BufferedImage.TYPE_INT_RGB
+   }
+
+   test("subimage falls back to the default type for TYPE_CUSTOM sources") {
+      // TYPE_CUSTOM (0) cannot be passed to the BufferedImage(w, h, type)
+      // constructor, so the crop must fall back to the default ARGB type.
+      val cm = ComponentColorModel(
+         ColorSpace.getInstance(ColorSpace.CS_sRGB),
+         intArrayOf(8, 8, 8),
+         false,
+         false,
+         Transparency.OPAQUE,
+         DataBuffer.TYPE_BYTE
+      )
+      val raster = cm.createCompatibleWritableRaster(4, 4)
+      val custom = BufferedImage(cm, raster, false, null)
+      custom.type shouldBe BufferedImage.TYPE_CUSTOM
+
+      val image = ImmutableImage.wrapAwt(custom)
+      image.type shouldBe BufferedImage.TYPE_CUSTOM
+      val sub = image.subimage(0, 0, 2, 2)
+      sub.type shouldBe ImmutableImage.DEFAULT_DATA_TYPE
+   }
 })

author: Sam
message: Bulk-read pixels in ColorThief.getPixelsSlow (#537)
patch:
commit 0a9a626ba4e6f3a3d23fb6c3148043f4abfdac5d
Author: Sam <sam@sksamuel.com>
Date:   Thu May 28 21:27:17 2026 -0500

    Bulk-read pixels in ColorThief.getPixelsSlow (#537)
    
    getPixelsSlow sampled pixels with a scalar BufferedImage.getRGB(col, row)
    per iteration, plus an integer divide and modulo to derive col/row from
    the flat index.
    
    Read the whole raster once with a bulk getRGB and index it directly. The
    flat index i already maps to the row-major buffer (i = row*width + col),
    so rgbAll[i] equals the previous getRGB(i % width, i / width), and the
    sampling stride, white-filter and r/g/b extraction are unchanged. Both
    scalar and bulk getRGB apply the colour model identically, so this stays
    correct for the non-standard colour models this slow path exists for.
    
    Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
---
 .../src/main/java/thirdparty/colorthief/ColorThief.java        | 10 +++++++---
 1 file changed, 7 insertions(+), 3 deletions(-)

diff --git a/scrimage-core/src/main/java/thirdparty/colorthief/ColorThief.java b/scrimage-core/src/main/java/thirdparty/colorthief/ColorThief.java
index 1b89f61a..c5323106 100644
--- a/scrimage-core/src/main/java/thirdparty/colorthief/ColorThief.java
+++ b/scrimage-core/src/main/java/thirdparty/colorthief/ColorThief.java
@@ -305,10 +305,14 @@ public class ColorThief {
       int[][] res = new int[numRegardedPixels][];
       int r, g, b;
 
+      // Read the whole raster once instead of a slow scalar getRGB(col, row)
+      // per sampled pixel. The flat index i maps directly to the row-major
+      // buffer (i = row * width + col), so rgbAll[i] equals getRGB(i % width,
+      // i / width).
+      int[] rgbAll = sourceImage.getRGB(0, 0, width, height, null, 0, width);
+
       for (int i = 0; i < pixelCount; i += quality) {
-         int row = i / width;
-         int col = i % width;
-         int rgb = sourceImage.getRGB(col, row);
+         int rgb = rgbAll[i];
 
          r = (rgb >> 16) & 0xFF;
          g = (rgb >> 8) & 0xFF;

patch:

diff --git a/.github/workflows/benchmarks.yml b/.github/workflows/benchmarks.yml
deleted file mode 100644
index d96c7b37..00000000
--- a/.github/workflows/benchmarks.yml
+++ /dev/null
@@ -1,72 +0,0 @@
-name: benchmarks
-
-on:
-  push:
-    branches:
-      - main
-      - master
-    paths-ignore:
-      - 'docs/**'
-      - '*.md'
-      - 'mkdocs.yml'
-  workflow_dispatch:
-    inputs:
-      include:
-        description: 'JMH benchmark regex filter (e.g. ResizeBenchmarks)'
-        required: false
-        default: ''
-
-jobs:
-  build:
-    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
-    timeout-minutes: 60
-
-    steps:
-      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
-
-      - name: Run benchmarks
-        run: |
-          if [ -n "${{ inputs.include }}" ]; then
-            ./gradlew :scrimage-benchmarks:jmh -Pjmh.includes='${{ inputs.include }}'
-          else
-            ./gradlew :scrimage-benchmarks:jmh
-          fi
-
-      - name: Render results to step summary
-        if: ${{ !cancelled() }}
-        run: |
-          set -euo pipefail
-          RESULTS=scrimage-benchmarks/build/results/jmh/results.json
-          {
-            echo "## JMH Benchmark Results"
-            echo
-            echo "Commit: \`${{ github.sha }}\`"
-            echo
-            if [ ! -s "$RESULTS" ]; then
-              echo "_No JMH results produced at \`$RESULTS\`._"
-              exit 0
-            fi
-            echo "| Benchmark | Mode | Score | Error | Unit |"
-            echo "|---|---|---:|---:|---|"
-            jq -r '
-              .[]
-              | [
-                  (.benchmark | sub("^com\\.sksamuel\\.scrimage\\."; "")),
-                  .mode,
-                  (.primaryMetric.score | . * 1000 | round / 1000 | tostring),
-                  ("± " + (.primaryMetric.scoreError | . * 1000 | round / 1000 | tostring)),
-                  .primaryMetric.scoreUnit
-                ]
-              | "| " + join(" | ") + " |"
-            ' "$RESULTS"
-          } >> "$GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY"
-
-      - name: Upload raw JMH results
-        if: ${{ !cancelled() }}
-        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v7
-        with:
-          name: jmh-results
-          path: scrimage-benchmarks/build/results/jmh/

author: Sam
message: Render JMH results in the benchmarks workflow (#542)
patch:
commit 04cc2fe660d3e7e64b9e347b26257457dcb390ae
Author: Sam <sam@sksamuel.com>
Date:   Thu May 28 21:25:47 2026 -0500

    Render JMH results in the benchmarks workflow (#542)
    
    Mirror the jmh workflow: after running the benchmarks, render
    results.json into a markdown table on the job step summary and upload
    the raw results as an artifact. Also add a workflow_dispatch include
    filter and a 60-minute timeout, and run the module task directly
    (:scrimage-benchmarks:jmh) so the results file is produced at the
    expected path.
    
    Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
---
 .github/workflows/benchmarks.yml | 49 +++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++-
 1 file changed, 48 insertions(+), 1 deletion(-)

diff --git a/.github/workflows/benchmarks.yml b/.github/workflows/benchmarks.yml
index 03622dcb..d96c7b37 100644
--- a/.github/workflows/benchmarks.yml
+++ b/.github/workflows/benchmarks.yml
@@ -10,16 +10,63 @@ on:
       - '*.md'
       - 'mkdocs.yml'
   workflow_dispatch:
+    inputs:
+      include:
+        description: 'JMH benchmark regex filter (e.g. ResizeBenchmarks)'
+        required: false
+        default: ''
 
 jobs:
   build:
     runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+    timeout-minutes: 60
 
     steps:
       - uses: actions/checkout@v6
 
       - name: Run benchmarks
-        run: ./gradlew jmh
+        run: |
+          if [ -n "${{ inputs.include }}" ]; then
+            ./gradlew :scrimage-benchmarks:jmh -Pjmh.includes='${{ inputs.include }}'
+          else
+            ./gradlew :scrimage-benchmarks:jmh
+          fi
+
+      - name: Render results to step summary
+        if: ${{ !cancelled() }}
+        run: |
+          set -euo pipefail
+          RESULTS=scrimage-benchmarks/build/results/jmh/results.json
+          {
+            echo "## JMH Benchmark Results"
+            echo
+            echo "Commit: \`${{ github.sha }}\`"
+            echo
+            if [ ! -s "$RESULTS" ]; then
+              echo "_No JMH results produced at \`$RESULTS\`._"
+              exit 0
+            fi
+            echo "| Benchmark | Mode | Score | Error | Unit |"
+            echo "|---|---|---:|---:|---|"
+            jq -r '
+              .[]
+              | [
+                  (.benchmark | sub("^com\\.sksamuel\\.scrimage\\."; "")),
+                  .mode,
+                  (.primaryMetric.score | . * 1000 | round / 1000 | tostring),
+                  ("± " + (.primaryMetric.scoreError | . * 1000 | round / 1000 | tostring)),
+                  .primaryMetric.scoreUnit
+                ]
+              | "| " + join(" | ") + " |"
+            ' "$RESULTS"
+          } >> "$GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY"
+
+      - name: Upload raw JMH results
+        if: ${{ !cancelled() }}
+        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v7
+        with:
+          name: jmh-results
+          path: scrimage-benchmarks/build/results/jmh/
 
 env:
   GRADLE_OPTS: -Dorg.gradle.configureondemand=true -Dorg.gradle.parallel=false -Dorg.gradle.jvmargs="-Xmx3g -XX:+HeapDumpOnOutOfMemoryError -Dfile.encoding=UTF-8"

author: Sam
message: Add invariant tests for AwtImage bulk-ARGB accessors (#541)
patch:
commit 36df1d131aa8d256745b3631b782202130adafd4
Author: Sam <sam@sksamuel.com>
Date:   Thu May 28 21:14:23 2026 -0500

    Add invariant tests for AwtImage bulk-ARGB accessors (#541)
    
    Property-style invariants asserting that the five views over the same
    pixel buffer agree:
    
    - argbints() == awt().getRGB(0, 0, w, h, ...)
    - argbints()[i] == pixels(0, 0, w, h)[i].toARGBInt()
    - argb()[i] == [alpha, red, green, blue] of the pixel at flat index i
    - rgb()[i] == [red, green, blue] of the pixel at flat index i
    - colors()[i].toARGBInt() == argbints()[i]
    
    Covers 1xN/Nx1/odd/even dimensions across TYPE_INT_ARGB, TYPE_INT_RGB
    and TYPE_4BYTE_ABGR. A wrong channel order in argb()/rgb(), an
    off-by-one between the bulk and pixel views, or an alpha-stripping bug
    in any one path would surface here.
    
    Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
---
 .../ArgbAccessorConsistencyInvariantTest.kt        | 112 +++++++++++++++++++++
 1 file changed, 112 insertions(+)

diff --git a/scrimage-tests/src/test/kotlin/com/sksamuel/scrimage/core/invariants/ArgbAccessorConsistencyInvariantTest.kt b/scrimage-tests/src/test/kotlin/com/sksamuel/scrimage/core/invariants/ArgbAccessorConsistencyInvariantTest.kt
new file mode 100644
index 00000000..1f7a4572
--- /dev/null
+++ b/scrimage-tests/src/test/kotlin/com/sksamuel/scrimage/core/invariants/ArgbAccessorConsistencyInvariantTest.kt
@@ -0,0 +1,112 @@
+package com.sksamuel.scrimage.core.invariants
+
+import com.sksamuel.scrimage.ImmutableImage
+import io.kotest.core.spec.style.FunSpec
+import io.kotest.matchers.shouldBe
+import java.awt.image.BufferedImage
+
+/**
+ * Property-style invariants for the bulk-ARGB accessors on AwtImage.
+ *
+ *   argbints()  ==  awt().getRGB(0, 0, w, h, null, 0, w)
+ *   argbints()[i]  ==  pixels(0, 0, w, h)[i].toARGBInt()
+ *   argb()[i]   ==  [pixel.alpha, pixel.red, pixel.green, pixel.blue]
+ *                   for the pixel at flat index i
+ *   rgb()[i]    ==  [pixel.red, pixel.green, pixel.blue]
+ *   colors()[i].toARGBInt()  ==  argbints()[i]
+ *
+ * These five views (argbints, argb, rgb, colors, pixels) all read the
+ * same buffer through different code paths. A divergence — wrong
+ * channel order in argb()/rgb(), an off-by-one between the bulk and
+ * pixel views, an alpha-stripping bug in any one path — would
+ * surface here as a contract violation.
+ */
+class ArgbAccessorConsistencyInvariantTest : FunSpec({
+
+   fun seed(width: Int, height: Int, type: Int): ImmutableImage {
+      val buf = BufferedImage(width, height, type)
+      for (y in 0 until height) for (x in 0 until width) {
+         val r = (x * 11) and 0xFF
+         val g = (y * 13) and 0xFF
+         val b = ((x + y) * 7) and 0xFF
+         buf.setRGB(x, y, (0xFF shl 24) or (r shl 16) or (g shl 8) or b)
+      }
+      return ImmutableImage.wrapAwt(buf)
+   }
+
+   val cases = listOf(
+      Triple(1, 1, BufferedImage.TYPE_INT_ARGB),
+      Triple(8, 1, BufferedImage.TYPE_INT_ARGB),
+      Triple(1, 8, BufferedImage.TYPE_INT_ARGB),
+      Triple(7, 13, BufferedImage.TYPE_INT_ARGB),
+      Triple(7, 13, BufferedImage.TYPE_INT_RGB),
+      Triple(7, 13, BufferedImage.TYPE_4BYTE_ABGR),
+      Triple(32, 16, BufferedImage.TYPE_INT_ARGB),
+   )
+
+   test("argbints() matches a fresh awt.getRGB bulk fetch") {
+      for ((w, h, t) in cases) {
+         val img = seed(w, h, t)
+         val direct = img.awt().getRGB(0, 0, w, h, null, 0, w)
+         val bulk = img.argbints()
+         bulk.contentEquals(direct) shouldBe true
+      }
+   }
+
+   test("argbints()[i] equals pixels(0, 0, w, h)[i].toARGBInt()") {
+      for ((w, h, t) in cases) {
+         val img = seed(w, h, t)
+         val ints = img.argbints()
+         val pix = img.pixels(0, 0, w, h)
+         ints.size shouldBe pix.size
+         for (i in ints.indices) {
+            ints[i] shouldBe pix[i].toARGBInt()
+         }
+      }
+   }
+
+   test("argb()[i] returns [alpha, red, green, blue] for the pixel at flat index i") {
+      for ((w, h, t) in cases) {
+         val img = seed(w, h, t)
+         val argb = img.argb()
+         val pix = img.pixels(0, 0, w, h)
+         argb.size shouldBe pix.size
+         for (i in argb.indices) {
+            val row = argb[i]
+            row.size shouldBe 4
+            row[0] shouldBe pix[i].alpha()
+            row[1] shouldBe pix[i].red()
+            row[2] shouldBe pix[i].green()
+            row[3] shouldBe pix[i].blue()
+         }
+      }
+   }
+
+   test("rgb()[i] returns [red, green, blue] for the pixel at flat index i") {
+      for ((w, h, t) in cases) {
+         val img = seed(w, h, t)
+         val rgb = img.rgb()
+         val pix = img.pixels(0, 0, w, h)
+         rgb.size shouldBe pix.size
+         for (i in rgb.indices) {
+            val row = rgb[i]
+            row.size shouldBe 3
+            row[0] shouldBe pix[i].red()
+            row[1] shouldBe pix[i].green()
+            row[2] shouldBe pix[i].blue()
+         }
+      }
+   }
+
+   test("colors()[i].toARGBInt() equals argbints()[i]") {
+      for ((w, h, t) in cases) {
+         val img = seed(w, h, t)
+         val colours = img.colors()
+         val ints = img.argbints()
+         colours.size shouldBe ints.size
+         for (i in colours.indices) {
+            colours[i].toARGBInt() shouldBe ints[i]
+         }
+      }
+   }
+})

author: Sam
message: Fix HSBAdjustFilter hue wrap using 2π on a normalised hue (#490)
patch:
commit 54373a6e1768364ee35d9b32b7741780d650ad6b
Author: Sam <sam@sksamuel.com>
Date:   Thu May 28 14:00:01 2026 -0500

    Fix HSBAdjustFilter hue wrap using 2π on a normalised hue (#490)
    
    Color.RGBtoHSB produces hue in [0, 1), but the negative-hue wrap loop
    added Math.PI*2 (~6.28). Adding ~6.28 to a normalised hue and feeding
    it to HSBtoRGB collapses to (hue + ~0.283) mod 1 — an arbitrary
    offset, not a wrap. For pure red (hue=0) with hFactor=-0.1 the
    expected post-shift hue is 0.9 (magenta); the bug produced ~0.18
    (orange).
    
    Replace the loop with `hsb[0] = hsb[0] - floor(hsb[0])` so any
    magnitude of negative drift normalises into [0, 1) in O(1).
    
    Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
---
 .../thirdparty/jhlabs/image/HSBAdjustFilter.java   |  9 +++-
 .../com/sksamuel/scrimage/filter/HSBHueWrapTest.kt | 54 ++++++++++++++++++++++
 2 files changed, 61 insertions(+), 2 deletions(-)

diff --git a/scrimage-filters/src/main/java/thirdparty/jhlabs/image/HSBAdjustFilter.java b/scrimage-filters/src/main/java/thirdparty/jhlabs/image/HSBAdjustFilter.java
index 58099537..5c203816 100644
--- a/scrimage-filters/src/main/java/thirdparty/jhlabs/image/HSBAdjustFilter.java
+++ b/scrimage-filters/src/main/java/thirdparty/jhlabs/image/HSBAdjustFilter.java
@@ -65,8 +65,13 @@ public class HSBAdjustFilter extends PointFilter {
 		int b = rgb & 0xff;
 		Color.RGBtoHSB(r, g, b, hsb);
 		hsb[0] += hFactor;
-		while (hsb[0] < 0)
-			hsb[0] += Math.PI*2;
+		// Color.RGBtoHSB produces hue normalised to [0, 1), not [0, 2π).
+		// The previous wrap added Math.PI*2 (~6.28), so a negative hue
+		// was nudged into the positive range but at a meaningless offset
+		// — for hue=0 with hFactor=-0.1, expected post-shift hue 0.9,
+		// actual ~0.18. Wrap with a single fract() so any magnitude of
+		// negative drift normalises in O(1) rather than O(n) loops.
+		hsb[0] = hsb[0] - (float) Math.floor(hsb[0]);
 		hsb[1] += sFactor;
 		if (hsb[1] < 0)
 			hsb[1] = 0;
diff --git a/scrimage-filters/src/test/kotlin/com/sksamuel/scrimage/filter/HSBHueWrapTest.kt b/scrimage-filters/src/test/kotlin/com/sksamuel/scrimage/filter/HSBHueWrapTest.kt
new file mode 100644
index 00000000..f680b643
--- /dev/null
+++ b/scrimage-filters/src/test/kotlin/com/sksamuel/scrimage/filter/HSBHueWrapTest.kt
@@ -0,0 +1,54 @@
+package com.sksamuel.scrimage.filter
+
+import com.sksamuel.scrimage.ImmutableImage
+import io.kotest.core.spec.style.FunSpec
+import io.kotest.matchers.comparables.shouldBeLessThan
+
+/**
+ * Regression: HSBAdjustFilter's negative-hue wrap added Math.PI*2 to
+ * a hue stored in `[0, 1)`. Adding ~6.28 to a normalised hue and
+ * handing it to HSBtoRGB (which internally takes `h - floor(h)`)
+ * produced an arbitrary offset of ~0.283, not the intended wrap
+ * back into [0, 1). For pure red (hue=0) with hFactor=-0.1 the
+ * expected post-shift hue is 0.9 (a magenta); the bug produced a
+ * hue ~0.18 (orange).
+ *
+ * The fix replaces the loop with `hsb[0] = hsb[0] - floor(hsb[0])`.
+ */
+class HSBHueWrapTest : FunSpec({
+
+   fun pixelDist(a: com.sksamuel.scrimage.pixels.Pixel, b: com.sksamuel.scrimage.pixels.Pixel): Double {
+      val dr = (a.red() - b.red()).toDouble()
+      val dg = (a.green() - b.green()).toDouble()
+      val db = (a.blue() - b.blue()).toDouble()
+      return kotlin.math.sqrt(dr * dr + dg * dg + db * db)
+   }
+
+   test("negative hFactor of -0.1 from pure red lands near magenta, not orange") {
+      // Pure red is at hue 0. With a -0.1 hue shift the expected hue
+      // is 0.9, which in RGB is a magenta — approximately (255, 0, 153).
+      // Pre-fix the result was an orange-ish (255, ~70, 0).
+      val red = ImmutableImage.filled(8, 8, java.awt.Color.RED)
+      val shifted = red.filter(HSBFilter(-0.1f, 0f, 0f))
+
+      val out = shifted.pixel(0, 0)
+      val expectedMagenta = com.sksamuel.scrimage.pixels.Pixel(0, 0, 255, 0, 153, 255)
+      val buggyOrange = com.sksamuel.scrimage.pixels.Pixel(0, 0, 255, 70, 0, 255)
+
+      // The post-fix output should be much closer to the expected
+      // magenta than to the orange that the pre-fix code produced.
+      pixelDist(out, expectedMagenta) shouldBeLessThan pixelDist(out, buggyOrange)
+   }
+
+   test("hFactor of +1.0 returns to the same colour (full hue cycle)") {
+      // A full +1.0 hue cycle should round-trip the image: hue + 1
+      // mod 1 = hue. With the buggy loop +Math.PI*2 wrap, this only
+      // touched the negative path, but the round-trip exercises the
+      // floor() branch too.
+      val red = ImmutableImage.filled(8, 8, java.awt.Color.RED)
+      val rotated = red.filter(HSBFilter(1.0f, 0f, 0f))
+      val out = rotated.pixel(0, 0)
+      val rgb = com.sksamuel.scrimage.pixels.Pixel(0, 0, 255, 0, 0, 255)
+      pixelDist(out, rgb) shouldBeLessThan 5.0
+   }
+})

author: Sam
message: Validate bounds in AwtImage.patch to prevent silent row-wrap (#484)
patch:
commit 02406710579f6437343c1593152278cc6a771d38
Author: Sam <sam@sksamuel.com>
Date:   Thu May 28 13:59:31 2026 -0500

    Validate bounds in AwtImage.patch to prevent silent row-wrap (#484)
    
    `patch(x, y, patchWidth, patchHeight)` had no bounds check. The
    underlying helper does a contiguous System.arraycopy of `patchWidth`
    pixels per row from a row-major flat array, so if `x + patchWidth >
    width` the read silently spans into the next row — returning the
    wrong pixels with no error. If the read goes past the end of the
    source array, arraycopy throws ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException with
    no useful context.
    
    Validate at the public entry point. Callers from inside `patches()`
    already iterate within bounds and are unaffected.
    
    Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
---
 .../main/java/com/sksamuel/scrimage/AwtImage.java  |  9 ++++
 .../com/sksamuel/scrimage/core/PatchBoundsTest.kt  | 63 ++++++++++++++++++++++
 2 files changed, 72 insertions(+)

diff --git a/scrimage-core/src/main/java/com/sksamuel/scrimage/AwtImage.java b/scrimage-core/src/main/java/com/sksamuel/scrimage/AwtImage.java
index fc406baf..10cbe8e9 100644
--- a/scrimage-core/src/main/java/com/sksamuel/scrimage/AwtImage.java
+++ b/scrimage-core/src/main/java/com/sksamuel/scrimage/AwtImage.java
@@ -402,9 +402,18 @@ public class AwtImage {
    }
 
    public Pixel[] patch(int x, int y, int patchWidth, int patchHeight) {
+      if (x < 0 || y < 0 || patchWidth < 0 || patchHeight < 0
+            || x + patchWidth > width || y + patchHeight > height) {
+         throw new IllegalArgumentException(
+            "Patch (" + x + "," + y + " " + patchWidth + "x" + patchHeight + ") falls outside image bounds " + width + "x" + height);
+      }
       return patchFrom(pixels(), x, y, patchWidth, patchHeight);
    }
 
+   // Internal helper, callers (this::patch, this::patches) are responsible
+   // for staying inside [0, width] × [0, height]. arraycopy below copies
+   // patchWidth contiguous Pixels per row from a row-major flat array, so
+   // an out-of-range x or width would silently span row boundaries.
    private Pixel[] patchFrom(Pixel[] source, int x, int y, int patchWidth, int patchHeight) {
       Pixel[] patch = new Pixel[patchWidth * patchHeight];
       for (int i = 0; i < patchHeight; i++) {
diff --git a/scrimage-tests/src/test/kotlin/com/sksamuel/scrimage/core/PatchBoundsTest.kt b/scrimage-tests/src/test/kotlin/com/sksamuel/scrimage/core/PatchBoundsTest.kt
new file mode 100644
index 00000000..068253c9
--- /dev/null
+++ b/scrimage-tests/src/test/kotlin/com/sksamuel/scrimage/core/PatchBoundsTest.kt
@@ -0,0 +1,63 @@
+package com.sksamuel.scrimage.core
+
+import com.sksamuel.scrimage.ImmutableImage
+import com.sksamuel.scrimage.pixels.Pixel
+import io.kotest.assertions.throwables.shouldThrow
+import io.kotest.core.spec.style.FunSpec
+import io.kotest.matchers.shouldBe
+
+/**
+ * Regression: `AwtImage.patch(x, y, patchWidth, patchHeight)` had no
+ * bounds check. The internal helper does a contiguous `System.arraycopy`
+ * of `patchWidth` pixels per row from a row-major flat array; if
+ * `x + patchWidth > width` the copy silently spans into the next row,
+ * returning the wrong pixels rather than failing. If the read goes off
+ * the end of the source array the call throws ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException
+ * from inside arraycopy with no useful context.
+ */
+class PatchBoundsTest : FunSpec({
+
+   // 4x4 image where each pixel's red channel equals its flat index 0..15.
+   fun indexedImage(): ImmutableImage {
+      val pixels = Array(16) { i -> Pixel(i % 4, i / 4, i, 0, 0, 255) }
+      return ImmutableImage.create(4, 4, pixels)
+   }
+
+   test("in-bounds patch returns the correct pixels") {
+      val image = indexedImage()
+      val patch = image.patch(1, 1, 2, 2)
+      // (1,1)=5, (2,1)=6, (1,2)=9, (2,2)=10 — row-major
+      patch[0].red() shouldBe 5
+      patch[1].red() shouldBe 6
+      patch[2].red() shouldBe 9
+      patch[3].red() shouldBe 10
+   }
+
+   test("patch overflowing the right edge throws IllegalArgumentException") {
+      val image = indexedImage()
+      // x=3, patchWidth=2 → would silently span into the next row
+      // (returning pixels [3, 4, 7, 8] = right column of row 0 +
+      // left column of row 1).
+      shouldThrow<IllegalArgumentException> { image.patch(3, 0, 2, 2) }
+   }
+
+   test("patch overflowing the bottom edge throws IllegalArgumentException") {
+      val image = indexedImage()
+      // y=3, patchHeight=2 → row 4 doesn't exist; pre-fix this threw
+      // an opaque ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException from inside arraycopy.
+      shouldThrow<IllegalArgumentException> { image.patch(0, 3, 2, 2) }
+   }
+
+   test("patch with negative origin throws IllegalArgumentException") {
+      val image = indexedImage()
+      shouldThrow<IllegalArgumentException> { image.patch(-1, 0, 2, 2) }
+      shouldThrow<IllegalArgumentException> { image.patch(0, -1, 2, 2) }
+   }
+
+   test("patch matching the full image is allowed") {
+      val image = indexedImage()
+      val full = image.patch(0, 0, 4, 4)
+      full.size shouldBe 16
+      full[15].red() shouldBe 15
+   }
+})
